root_cause_explanation: The tests were flaky under the race detector due to unsafe sharing of mutable state across goroutines (e.g., reusing an outer-scope `err` variable and concurrency around sync primitives). This PR stabilizes it by avoiding these data races (use goroutine-local `err := ...` in the test and avoid copying/unsafe usage of sync.WaitGroup in the compactor).
fix_pattern: Avoid data races by using goroutine-local variables (e.g., `:=`) and not copying sync primitives; keep shared sync objects safely owned (e.g., WaitGroup pointer) when used concurrently.

evidence: files: store/localstore/kv.go, store/localstore/txn.go, util/codec/bytes.go, kv/union_store.go, store/localstore/compactor.go, store/localstore/boltdb/boltdb.go, store/localstore/snapshot.go, store/localstore/engine/engine.go, store/localstore/isolation_test.go
